steps.title: Identify the grammar issue
steps.content: The pronoun replaces "many historical newspapers" / "these rare items" — both plural antecedents. The blank needs a plural object pronoun.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
A: it — singular object pronoun. "People can access it" treats the newspapers as one unit, disagreeing with the plural antecedent. Eliminated.

B: them — plural object pronoun. "People around the world can access them" — agrees with "many historical newspapers." Correct.

C: this — singular demonstrative pronoun. Same number error as A. Eliminated.

D: that — singular demonstrative pronoun. Also singular; does not match the plural antecedent. Eliminated.
